chapters.title: Why RAG Exists
chapters.hook: Your AI is trained on yesterday's news. RAG is how you plug it into today.
chapters.outcome: Explain what RAG is and why it solves the hallucination problem

chapters.title: Data Ingestion & Chunking
chapters.hook: You can't feed a library into an AI all at once. Chunking is how you cut it into pieces the AI can actually use.
chapters.outcome: Split any document into well-sized chunks and understand the tradeoffs

chapters.title: Embeddings & The Vector Space
chapters.hook: An embedding is just a list of numbers that captures the meaning of a sentence. Similar sentences have similar numbers.
chapters.outcome: Understand what embeddings are and use them to find similar text

chapters.title: Vector Databases
chapters.hook: A vector database is just a database that's really fast at the question: 'What's most similar to this?'
chapters.outcome: Choose and use the right vector database for your use case

chapters.title: Retrieval Strategies
chapters.hook: Searching by meaning is just the beginning. The difference between a toy demo and a real product is how smart your search is.
chapters.outcome: Implement hybrid search, MMR, and re-ranking strategies

chapters.title: The Prompt Layer
chapters.hook: Retrieved chunks are just raw ingredients. The prompt is the recipe that tells the AI what to cook.
chapters.outcome: Write effective RAG prompts with citation formatting

chapters.title: Evaluation
chapters.hook: Building a RAG system is easy. Knowing if it actually works is the hard part.
chapters.outcome: Measure faithfulness, relevancy, precision, and recall with RAGAS

chapters.title: Advanced RAG Patterns
chapters.hook: Standard RAG is a knife. These patterns are the rest of the kitchen.
chapters.outcome: Choose the right advanced pattern for your specific problem
